SEARCH
Package com.comp2042.service.gameloop

Interface GameService

All Known Implementing Classes:
GameServiceImpl
public interface GameService
Service layer interface for game logic operations (Service Layer Pattern). Provides clean abstraction between controllers and game implementation. Handles event processing, state management, and timing control.
Author:
Dong, Jia.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Board
    getBoard()
    Gets the game board instance.
    int[][]
    getNextBrick()
    Gets the next brick shape data for preview display.
    Score
    getScore()
    Gets the score tracker instance.
    boolean
    isGameOver()
    Checks if the game has ended.
    DownData
    processDownEvent(MoveEvent event)
    Processes a DOWN movement event.
    ViewData
    processHoldEvent(MoveEvent event)
    Processes a hold event.
    ViewData
    processLeftEvent(MoveEvent event)
    Processes a LEFT movement event.
    ViewData
    processRightEvent(MoveEvent event)
    Processes a RIGHT movement event.
    ViewData
    processRotateCCWEvent(MoveEvent event)
    Processes a counterclockwise rotation event.
    ViewData
    processRotateEvent(MoveEvent event)
    Processes a clockwise rotation event.
    void
    setDropSpeed(int speed)
    Sets the drop speed for automatic brick falling.
    void
    startNewGame()
    Starts a new game session.

  • Method Details

    • getBoard

      Board getBoard()
      Gets the game board instance.
      Returns:
      the game board

    • getScore

      Score getScore()
      Gets the score tracker instance.
      Returns:
      the score tracker

    • processDownEvent

      DownData processDownEvent(MoveEvent event)
      Processes a DOWN movement event. Moves the falling brick down one position and handles landing logic.
      Parameters:
      event - the move event containing type and source
      Returns:
      down data containing view information and row-clearing results

    • processLeftEvent

      ViewData processLeftEvent(MoveEvent event)
      Processes a LEFT movement event. Moves the falling brick one position to the left.
      Parameters:
      event - the move event containing type and source
      Returns:
      view data containing updated brick position and shape

    • processRightEvent

      ViewData processRightEvent(MoveEvent event)
      Processes a RIGHT movement event. Moves the falling brick one position to the right.
      Parameters:
      event - the move event containing type and source
      Returns:
      view data containing updated brick position and shape

    • processRotateEvent

      ViewData processRotateEvent(MoveEvent event)
      Processes a clockwise rotation event. Rotates the falling brick 90 degrees clockwise.
      Parameters:
      event - the move event containing type and source
      Returns:
      view data containing updated brick position and rotated shape

    • processRotateCCWEvent

      ViewData processRotateCCWEvent(MoveEvent event)
      Processes a counterclockwise rotation event. Rotates the falling brick 90 degrees counterclockwise.
      Parameters:
      event - the move event containing type and source
      Returns:
      view data containing updated brick position and rotated shape

    • setDropSpeed

      void setDropSpeed(int speed)
      Sets the drop speed for automatic brick falling. Lower values result in faster falling.
      Parameters:
      speed - the new drop speed in milliseconds between automatic drops

    • startNewGame

      void startNewGame()
      Starts a new game session. Resets the board, score, and spawns the first brick.

    • isGameOver

      boolean isGameOver()
      Checks if the game has ended. Game over occurs when a new brick cannot be spawned.
      Returns:
      true if the game is over, false otherwise

    • processHoldEvent

      ViewData processHoldEvent(MoveEvent event)
      Processes a hold event. Swaps the current brick with the held brick, or stores the current brick if none is held.
      Parameters:
      event - the move event containing type and source
      Returns:
      view data containing updated brick position and hold state

    • getNextBrick

      int[][] getNextBrick()
      Gets the next brick shape data for preview display.
      Returns:
      the next brick shape matrix, or null if unavailable
